title: polypodium vulgare subsp. rotundatum
intro: Polypodium vulgare subsp. rotundatum, also known as the polypody or round-leaf polypody, is a subspecies of fern native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its round or kidney-shaped fronds. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of polypodium vulgare subsp. rotundatum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
